revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = National Historic Site | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = }} {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial tumulii]] located in what is now part of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata prefecture, on the alluvial delta of the Inada River. The site was discovered in 1929, and excavation surveys were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tombs, Site B to the east was found to have eight tombs, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tombs. All of the ''kofun'' were dome-shaped tumuli, and contained lateral burial chambers ranging from 70 to 120 cm in width. Most had a diameter of four to five meters.
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an archaeological park, and has a small museum to display some of the artefacts found.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://joetsukankonavi.jp/spot.php?id=388 Joetsu Tourist information page] {{ja}}

[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an archaeological park, and has a small museum to display some of the artifacts found.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://joetsukankonavi.jp/spot.php?id=388 Joetsu Tourist information page] {{ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were circular-type ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
== Gallery == <gallery> 宮口古墳群出土品-1.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3 and No.4 宮口古墳群出土品-2.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.4, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-3.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3, No.4, No.5, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-4.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口11号墳出土 装飾付大刀.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口古墳群出土品-5.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 and No.26 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category|Miyaguchi Kofun Group}} *[http://joetsukankonavi.jp/spot.php?id=388 Joetsu Tourist information page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/soshiki/maki-ku/maki-kouen.html Joetsu City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
== Gallery == <gallery> 宮口古墳群出土品-1.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3 and No.4 宮口古墳群出土品-2.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.4, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-3.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3, No.4, No.5, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-4.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口11号墳出土 装飾付大刀.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口古墳群出土品-5.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 and No.26 </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
== Gallery == <gallery> 宮口古墳群出土品-1.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3 and No.4 宮口古墳群出土品-2.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.4, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-3.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3, No.4, No.5, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-4.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口11号墳出土 装飾付大刀.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口古墳群出土品-5.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 and No.26 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
== Gallery == <gallery> 宮口古墳群出土品-1.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3 and No.4 宮口古墳群出土品-2.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.4, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-3.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3, No.4, No.5, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-4.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口11号墳出土 装飾付大刀.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口古墳群出土品-5.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 and No.26 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] to [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
== Gallery == <gallery> 宮口古墳群出土品-1.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3 and No.4 宮口古墳群出土品-2.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.4, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-3.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3, No.4, No.5, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-4.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口11号墳出土 装飾付大刀.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口古墳群出土品-5.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 and No.26 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
== Gallery == <gallery> 宮口古墳群出土品-1.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3 and No.4 宮口古墳群出土品-2.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.4, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-3.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3, No.4, No.5, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-4.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口11号墳出土 装飾付大刀.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口古墳群出土品-5.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 and No.26 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mounds]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
== Gallery == <gallery> 宮口古墳群出土品-1.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3 and No.4 宮口古墳群出土品-2.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.4, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-3.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.3, No.4, No.5, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-4.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口11号墳出土 装飾付大刀.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 宮口古墳群出土品-5.JPG|Excavated items from Tumulus No.11 and No.26 </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofuns]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Jōetsu, Niigata]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofuns]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Jōetsu, Niigata]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the [[alluvial fan]] of the Inada River near the [[Sea of Japan]]. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
[[Grave goods]] recovered include [[Sue ware]], straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from [[Takada Station (Niigata)|Takada Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
