<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyaguchi Kofun Group]][[en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group]][[ja:宮口古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyaguchi Kofun Group]][[en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group]][[ja:宮口古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Miyaguchi Kofun Group]][[en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group]][[ja:宮口古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg; File:Miyaguchi Kofun.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|37.093139|N|138.357028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|37.093333|N|138.3575|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu, Niigata|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Joetsu|WD=Q582289|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}}| region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofuns]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu, Niigata|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan|1=Alluvial fan}} of the Inada River near the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from {{ill|Takada Station (Niigata)|en|Takada Station (Niigata)|WD=Q3701518|lt=Takada Station|1=Takada Station (Niigata)}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Shin'etsu Main Line|en|Shin'etsu Main Line|WD=Q1038051|lt=Shin'etsu Main Line|1=Shin'etsu Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
== Gallery == <gallery> 宮口古墳群出土品-1.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.3 and No.4 宮口古墳群出土品-2.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.4, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-3.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.3, No.4, No.5, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-4.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.11 宮口11号墳出土 装飾付大刀.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.11 宮口古墳群出土品-5.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.11 and No.26 </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyaguchi Kofun Group]][[en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group]][[ja:宮口古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Miyaguchi Kofun Group]][[en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group]][[ja:宮口古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group]][[ja:宮口古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | native_name = 宮口古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Miyaguchi Kofun Group, gaikan-2.jpg; File:Miyaguchi Kofun.jpg| image_size = | alt = | caption = Miyaguchi Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miyaguchi Kofun Group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|37|05|36|N|138|21|27|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|37.093139|N|138.357028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|37.093333|N|138.3575|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu, Niigata|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Joetsu|WD=Q582289|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}}| region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 13199.56 sq meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 6th to early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1929 | excavations =1973 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Miyaguchi Kofun Group'''|宮口古墳群|Miyaguchi Kofun-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing three groups of late [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofuns]] located in the Miyaguchi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Jōetsu, Niigata|en|Jōetsu, Niigata|WD=Q582289|lt=Jōetsu, Niigata|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/205173 |title=宮口古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Miyaguchi Kofun Group is located in the Takada Plains of the Jōetsu region of Niigata Prefecture, on the {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan|1=Alluvial fan}} of the Inada River near the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}. The site was discovered in 1929, and [[archaeological excavation]]s were conducted from 1973. The site consists of three groups of ''kofun''. Site A was found to contain 18 tumuli, Site B to the east was found to have eight tumuli, and Site C located slightly to the south of Site A was found to have five tumuli. All of the ''kofun'' were {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), with a diameter of four to five meters. All of the tumuli are concentrated in a small area of approximately 1.5 hectare. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Each of the tumuli had lateral [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s ranging from 70 to 140 cm in width and around two meters in length, with the largest, No.11, having a length of 6.6 meters.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} recovered include {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, straight iron swords, parts of armor, horse fittings and items of jewellery. From these grave goods, it is estimated that these tombs were constructed from the middle of the 6th century to the middle of the 7th century.
The sites are open to the public as an open air [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]], and some of the artifacts found are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Maki History and Folklore Museum''' |牧歴史民俗資料| Maki rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}} on site It is located a ten-minute walk from the "Miyaguchi" bus stop on the Echigo Kotsu Bus from {{ill|Takada Station (Niigata)|en|Takada Station (Niigata)|WD=Q3701518|lt=Takada Station|1=Takada Station (Niigata)}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Shin'etsu Main Line|en|Shin'etsu Main Line|WD=Q1038051|lt=Shin'etsu Main Line|1=Shin'etsu Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
== Gallery == <gallery> 宮口古墳群出土品-1.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.3 and No.4 宮口古墳群出土品-2.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.4, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-3.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.3, No.4, No.5, No.26 and No.29 宮口古墳群出土品-4.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.11 宮口11号墳出土 装飾付大刀.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.11 宮口古墳群出土品-5.JPG|Excavated items from Kofun No.11 and No.26 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Miyaguchi Kofun Group}} *[http://joetsukankonavi.jp/spot.php?id=388 Joetsu Tourist information page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/soshiki/maki-ku/maki-kouen.html Joetsu City home page] {{in lang|ja}}
